discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The street of Fellini's La Dolce Vita — Jacobs examines it as a street with character beyond its fame.

narrative.summary: The street of Fellini's La Dolce Vita — Jacobs examines it as a street with character beyond its fame.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: Elegant curved boulevard climbing from Piazza Barberini, lined with American embassy, grand hotels, pavement cafés under plane trees. The 1950s glamour is still present in the architecture even if the scene has moved on. The Capuchin Crypt at the bottom adds a macabre footnote.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
